questions.question_text: Describe your firm's investment strategy and philosophy.
questions.answer_text: Granite Peak Capital pursues a concentrated value investment strategy focused on U.S. small and mid-cap companies trading below intrinsic value. Philosophy rooted in fundamental bottom-up analysis with a focus on companies with durable competitive advantages, strong free cash flow generation, and capable management teams. Typically hold 25-35 positions with a 3-5 year investment horizon.

questions.question_text: What is your target market segment, geography, and sector focus?
questions.answer_text: U.S. small and mid-cap equities ($500M - $10B market cap). Primarily domestic (85-95% U.S.). Opportunistic across sectors, with historical concentrations in industrials, consumer staples, healthcare, and financial services.

questions.question_text: Describe your investment process from sourcing to exit.
questions.answer_text: Five stages: (1) Sourcing via quantitative screens, industry contacts, thematic research (~500 companies/yr), (2) Initial Review narrows to ~100, (3) Deep DD with modeling, management meetings, reference checks (4-8 weeks, ~30 investments), (4) Portfolio Construction at 2-4% initial positions, (5) Monitoring & Exit on thesis completion/invalidation.

questions.question_text: What is your target fund size and investment period?
questions.answer_text: Fund IV target: $2.5B. Hard cap: $3.0B. Evergreen structure with quarterly liquidity (45-day notice). Soft lock: 1 year.

questions.question_text: What is your typical deal size range?
questions.answer_text: $50M to $150M at cost. Initial positions typically $75M-$100M.

questions.question_text: Describe your sourcing process and competitive advantages in deal origination.
questions.answer_text: Three channels: (1) Proprietary quantitative screens, (2) Deep industry relationships (18 years, senior analysts avg 12+ yrs sector coverage), (3) Thematic research pipeline (post-spin-off situations, business transformations).

questions.question_text: What is your due diligence process for evaluating potential investments?
questions.answer_text: Structured 4-8 week process: Week 1-2 financial model construction, Week 2-3 management meetings, Week 3-4 customer/supplier reference checks (min 5 each), Week 4-6 site visits, Week 5-8 IC presentation. Unanimous IC approval required.

questions.question_text: Describe your portfolio construction approach, including diversification targets.
questions.answer_text: 25-35 positions. Max single position: 7% at cost, 10% at market. Max sector: 30%. Cash: 2-5% target (0-10% range). Active share target: >90% vs. Russell 2000 Value.

questions.question_text: What is your approach to value creation post-investment?
questions.answer_text: Value creation through security selection. Constructive engagement with management on capital allocation, cost structure, strategic direction. 3 activist campaigns in past 10 years, all resolved constructively.

questions.question_text: Describe your exit strategy and typical holding period.
questions.answer_text: Avg holding period: 3.2 years (6 months to 8+ years). Sell triggers: thesis completion (60%), better opportunity (25%), thesis invalidation (15%).

questions.question_text: Describe your investment committee process and approval requirements.
questions.answer_text: Every investment requires unanimous IC approval. Structured 4-8 week DD process culminating in IC presentation and debate.

questions.question_text: What is the maximum single investment as a percentage of the fund?
questions.answer_text: 7% at cost, 10% at market.

questions.question_text: Provide an organizational chart.
questions.answer_text: 42 employees total. Investment Team (18): Robert Chen CFA (Managing Partner & CIO, founded 2008, 26 yrs), Sarah Martinez (Partner, Senior PM, joined 2010, 22 yrs), David Park CFA (Partner, Senior Analyst, joined 2011, 19 yrs), 6 Senior Analysts, 5 Analysts, 4 Research Associates. Operations & Compliance (14). Client Service (6). Technology (4).

questions.question_text: How many total employees does the firm have?
questions.answer_text: 42 total as of December 31, 2025. Investment: 18, Operations & Compliance: 14, Client Service: 6, Technology: 4.

questions.question_text: Describe the ownership structure.
questions.answer_text: 100% employee-owned. Robert Chen: 55%, Sarah Martinez: 20%, David Park: 15%, Employee pool: 10%.

questions.question_text: Has there been any change in ownership in the last 5 years?
questions.answer_text: No. Stable since 2018 when David Park elevated to Partner.

questions.question_text: Identify the key persons.
questions.answer_text: Robert Chen and Sarah Martinez. Key person event triggers suspension of new investments until LPAC convenes (within 30 days).

questions.question_text: Have any key investment professionals departed in the last 3 years?
questions.answer_text: No key person departures in last 3 years. No senior analyst departures since 2021.

questions.question_text: Describe the compensation structure.
questions.answer_text: Base salary + annual bonus (0-200% of base) + carried interest for Partners. Analysts in deferred compensation pool. All investment professionals required to invest min 50% of annual bonus in the fund.

questions.question_text: What is the GP commitment?
questions.answer_text: $75 million (3% of target fund size). Partners commit additional personal capital.

questions.question_text: Describe succession planning.
questions.answer_text: Formal plan since 2020. Sarah Martinez successor CIO. David Park successor Managing Partner. Both delegated increasing PM authority. External board advisor reviews annually.

questions.question_text: List all other funds or accounts managed.
questions.answer_text: Value Fund III: $2.1B (closed). Value Fund IV: $1.8B (current, target $2.5B). Institutional SMA: $400M across 5 accounts. Total firm AUM: $4.3B.

questions.question_text: Total AUM?
questions.answer_text: $4.3 billion as of December 31, 2025.

questions.question_text: Provide gross and net returns for each fund since inception.
Provide annualized returns net of all fees.
questions.answer_text: Fund IV: 1yr 14.2% (bench 8.7%, +5.5%), 3yr 12.8% (bench 7.9%, +4.9%), since inception 14.9% (bench 10.2%, +4.7%).

questions.question_text: What is the relevant benchmark?
questions.answer_text: Russell 2000 Value Index. Secondary: Russell 2500 Value Index.

questions.question_text: Performance during significant market downturns?
Maximum drawdown and recovery period?
questions.answer_text: Fund I: -28.1% (2008), 14 months. Fund II: -12.3% (2015-16), 6 months. Fund III: -22.8% (Q1 2020), 9 months. Fund IV: -11.4% (2022), 5 months.

questions.question_text: Has the track record been audited?
questions.answer_text: Yes. Audited annually by Ernst & Young LLP. GIPS-compliant.

questions.question_text: Are returns GIPS-compliant?
questions.answer_text: Yes. GIPS-compliant since 2010. Verified by ACA Performance Services.

questions.question_text: Management fee rate and basis?
questions.answer_text: 85 bps on NAV, calculated monthly, paid quarterly in arrears.

questions.question_text: Does the management fee step down?
questions.answer_text: Yes. First $250M: 85 bps, $250M-$500M: 75 bps, Above $500M: 65 bps. Blended rate for $500M: ~77 bps.

questions.question_text: Carried interest rate and preferred return?
questions.answer_text: 15% of net profits above a 6% annualized hurdle rate, high-water mark.

questions.question_text: List all fees and expenses borne by the fund.
questions.answer_text: Management fee 65-85 bps (tiered), Performance fee 15% (above 6% hurdle), Admin/Custody ~8 bps, Audit/Legal ~3 bps, Trading ~5 bps. Total TER ~100 bps.

questions.question_text: Total expense ratio?
questions.answer_text: Approximately 100 basis points (inclusive of management fee).

questions.question_text: Describe your risk management framework.
questions.answer_text: Three-lines-of-defense model: (1) Investment team position-level assessment, (2) Risk committee (CIO + COO + CCO) weekly review, (3) External risk consultant (Albourne) quarterly. Limits: max position 7%/10%, max sector 30%, max cash 10%, min 20 positions.

questions.question_text: Is risk management independent?
questions.answer_text: Partially. Risk committee includes COO and CCO (independent) alongside CIO. External consultant (Albourne Partners) fully independent.

questions.question_text: Any material risk limit breaches in past 5 years?
questions.answer_text: One technical breach Q3 2023: position reached 10.3% (limit 10%), trimmed within 5 business days. No sector/cash/diversification breaches.

questions.question_text: SEC registration and CRD number?
questions.answer_text: SEC-registered under Investment Advisers Act of 1940. CRD: 287456.

questions.question_text: Any regulatory investigations or enforcement actions?
questions.answer_text: No. No investigation, enforcement, or sanction from SEC, FINRA, any state regulator, or SRO.

questions.question_text: Describe the compliance program.
questions.answer_text: CCO Jennifer Walsh (18 yrs experience) reports to Managing Partner with dotted line to independent advisory board. Annual review by Dechert LLP. Quarterly testing: personal trading, gifts, political contributions, information barriers.

questions.question_text: Who is the auditor?
questions.answer_text: Ernst & Young LLP. Continuous since 2008. No auditor changes.

questions.question_text: Pending or threatened litigation?
questions.answer_text: None.

questions.question_text: Amended ADV disclosures for disciplinary events?
questions.answer_text: No.

questions.question_text: PRI signatory?
questions.answer_text: Yes. Since 2019. PRI Assessment: A+ (Strategy & Governance), A (Listed Equity).

questions.question_text: Sustainability policy?
questions.answer_text: Sustainability factors integrated into fundamental analysis as material risk factors. Assess governance quality, environmental liabilities, social license to operate. No blanket exclusions; decline investments where sustainability risks are material and unmitigable.

questions.question_text: Climate risk assessment approach?
questions.answer_text: TCFD-aligned assessment for all holdings. Physical and transition risk scores per position. Portfolio carbon footprint reported quarterly.

questions.question_text: Cybersecurity program?
questions.answer_text: SOC 2 Type II certified (annual). NIST CSF aligned. Annual pen testing by CrowdStrike. AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit. MFA required for all systems.

questions.question_text: Any cybersecurity incidents?
questions.answer_text: No material incidents or data breaches in firm history.

gaps.question_text: Quarterly return history for most recent 5 years?
Total investments and realized vs unrealized split?
Code of ethics and personal trading?

flags.description: DDQ is substantially complete (48 questions answered) but skips several ILPA questions in Track Record (attribution, quarterly returns, top/bottom investments) and Fees (waterfall, LPAC, fund term).
One technical risk limit breach in Q3 2023 (position exceeded 10% limit by 0.3%, trimmed within 5 days). Disclosed voluntarily -- positive signal.

follow_up_questions.question: Can you provide quarterly return history for Fund IV since inception?
What is the distribution waterfall for Fund IV?
Describe the firm's valuation methodology and frequency for unrealized positions.
What stress testing or scenario analysis is performed on the portfolio?
